title: Named Arguments
description: Pass parameters in any order you want.

slides.title: The Memory Game 🧠
slides.content:
## Order Confusion

Imagine a function `fun createCharacter(name: String, classType: String, weapon: String)`. 

When calling it: `createCharacter("Sword", "Mage", "Alex")`. 
Wait, did I get the order right? Will my Mage be named Sword? 

Relying to strict ordering leads to subtle bugs.

slides.title: Name your arguments! 🏷️
slides.content:
Kotlin allows you to explicitly name your arguments when calling the function.

Once you name them, **the order in which you pass them no longer matters!**

quizData.question: What is the primary benefit of Named Arguments?

options.text: It makes the function run faster.
It increases readability and prevents accidental misordering of parameters.

quizData.explanation: When you open someone else's code (or your own 6 months later), named arguments make it instantly obvious what each value represents.

slides.title: Combining Features ✨
slides.content:
Named Arguments work beautifully when combined with Default Arguments.

If a function has 10 parameters (9 with defaults), you can use Named Arguments to pick the exactly ONE you want to manually change, ignoring the rest.
